summary: Ukrainian forces reported strikes on logistics and radar sites on both sides of the Crimean bridge, including oil transport and S‑400 radars. Russian troops destroyed a Ukrainian bunker in Konstantinovka, Donetsk, using an anti‑tank mine. Multiple Shahed UAVs and Russian tactical aviation activity were observed over various Ukrainian regions.
key_points: Ukrainian President announced successful strikes on maritime logistics at the Crimean bridge, an oil depot in occupied Kerch, four S‑400 radar stations and two Pantsir complexes (Source: @UkraineNow/82089).
Russian forces of the "Southern" grouping eliminated a Ukrainian bunker in the Konstantinovka area of the DPR with a TM‑62 anti‑tank mine (Source: @tass_agency/381733).
Shahed UAVs were reported over Vyshenky/Bortnychi and over Kharkiv, indicating ongoing drone attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67976).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east and south‑east fronts, posing a threat to near‑frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
A postal terminal in a Kharkiv suburb was struck, damaging containers, trucks and the building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31053).
Russian MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles were launched from Savatske airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Power outages were reported in Crimea’s north‑west, central and south‑coastal energy zones after drone attacks on critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Russian forces shelled Sloviansk with multiple rocket launchers, injuring two women and one man (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces struck logistics and air‑defense assets on both sides of the Crimean bridge, hitting oil transport and S‑400 radars. Russian troops used an anti‑tank mine to destroy a Ukrainian bunker in Konstantinovka, Donetsk. Shahed UAVs and increased Russian tactical aviation activity continue to pressure Ukrainian front‑line regions.

points: Ukrainian forces hit maritime logistics on the Crimean bridge, an oil depot in occupied Kerch, four S‑400 radar stations and two Pantsir complexes, according to President Zelensky (Source: @UkraineNow/82089).
A postal terminal in a Kharkiv suburb was struck, damaging containers, trucks and the building, reported by the State Emergency Service of Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31053).
Shahed UAVs were observed over Vyshenky/Bortnychi, indicating ongoing drone attacks on Ukrainian territory (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67976).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east and south‑east directions, threatening near‑frontline oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
Russian "Southern" grouping destroyed a Ukrainian bunker in Konstantinovka, Donetsk, using a TM‑62 anti‑tank mine (Source: @tass_agency/381733).
Russian MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles launched from Savatske airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Russian forces shelled Sloviansk with multiple rocket launchers, damaging multi‑storey buildings and injuring two women (30 and 89) and a 31‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
Power outages in Crimea’s north‑west, central and south‑coastal energy zones were linked to drone attacks on Russian critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Shahed UAVs were reported over Kharkiv and its suburbs, including a strike on a postal terminal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31053; @mon1tor_ua/67976).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the east and south‑east axes, raising the threat to front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
Ukrainian forces claimed strikes on logistics and radar sites near the Crimean bridge, affecting oil transport and S‑400 radars (Source: @UkraineNow/82089).
Russian forces used a TM‑62 anti‑tank mine to destroy a Ukrainian bunker in Konstantinovka, Donetsk (Source: @tass_agency/381733).
Multiple Shahed UAV sightings were reported over northern regions, including Sumy, Chernihiv and Kyiv oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/65889; @kpszsu/65867; @kpszsu/65864).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east direction, posing a threat to front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
Ukrainian radar channels reported a MiG‑31K launch from Savatske, indicating high‑speed missile capability in the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Ukrainian forces observed Shahed UAVs heading toward Zhytomyr and Korosten from northern Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65861).
Ukrainian Shahed UAVs were reported over Kharkiv from the north, indicating continued drone pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65889).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the south‑east direction, threatening southern front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
A strike on a local gas station in Zaporizhzhia injured one person, reported by regional authorities (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
Ukrainian forces claimed strikes on oil logistics in occupied Kerch, part of the Crimean bridge network (Source: @UkraineNow/82089).
Russian MiG‑31K fighters equipped with Kinzhal hypersonic missiles launched from Savatske airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Russian tactical aviation activity was observed on north‑east, east and south‑east axes, increasing aerial threat to Ukrainian front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/65882; @kpszsu/65895).
Ukrainian Shahed UAV attacks were reported over multiple oblasts, including Vyshenky/Bortnychi and Kharkiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67976).
Ukrainian air defense faced overload from UAV swarms, as noted in a summary of major events (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).

key_developments: Ukrainian strikes hit oil logistics and S‑400 radars on both sides of the Crimean bridge, disrupting Russian supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/82089).
Russian "Southern" grouping eliminated a Ukrainian bunker in Konstantinovka with a TM‑62 anti‑tank mine (Source: @tass_agency/381733).
Shahed UAV attacks were recorded over Vyshenky/Bortnychi and Kharkiv, indicating persistent drone threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67976).
Russian MiG‑31K fighters carrying Kinzhal missiles launched from Savatske, showing high‑speed strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Power outages in Crimea’s energy zones were linked to drone attacks on Russian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on north‑east and south‑east fronts, raising aerial threat levels (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
Shelling of Sloviansk with multiple rocket launchers caused civilian injuries and property damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to employ precision munitions, such as anti‑tank mines and tactical aviation, to target Ukrainian positions in Donetsk and front‑line oblasts. Ukrainian Shahed UAV operations and strikes on Russian logistics, especially around the Crimean bridge, demonstrate a reciprocal escalation. The presence of Kinzhal‑armed MiG‑31K aircraft raises the risk of rapid, long‑range strikes against critical infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defense around key logistics hubs and the Crimean bridge area, while reporting increased civilian casualties from Russian rocket fire in occupied territories. Russian statements emphasize the use of advanced missile platforms and tactical air operations to pressure Ukrainian front‑line regions. Both sides are mobilizing additional UAV surveillance and counter‑UAV measures across multiple oblasts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed UAV flight paths shows a pattern of north‑to‑south trajectories targeting Kharkiv and Kyiv regions. Russian tactical aviation sorties concentrate on north‑east and south‑east axes, suggesting preparation for deeper strikes. The TM‑62 mine usage indicates continued reliance on low‑tech, high‑impact anti‑personnel weapons in contested zones.
